platform_summary: IDRE (Independent Dispute Resolution Entity) is a healthcare dispute resolution platform under the No Surprises Act. It manages disputes between healthcare providers (initiating parties) and health plans (non-initiating parties). Cases go through eligibility review, payment collection, RFI, arbitration, and closure. The platform handles payments via ACH/NACHA, tracks invoices, manages refunds, and generates 13+ standard reports.

case_statuses.INITIAL_ELIGIBILITY_REVIEW: First-stage human review queue — case enters system, eligibility specialist reviews basic qualification
case_statuses.PENDING_PAYMENTS: First payment collection phase — both parties have 10 business days to pay entity fees
case_statuses.PENDING_SECOND_PAYMENT: One party has paid, waiting for the second party's payment
case_statuses.FINAL_ELIGIBILITY_REVIEW: Both parties paid (P=2) — specialist performs final eligibility check
case_statuses.PENDING_INITIAL_RFI: Initial Request for Information sent to parties for additional documentation
case_statuses.PENDING_RFI: Subsequent RFI request — 5 business days for response
case_statuses.FINAL_ELIGIBILITY_COMPLETED: Passed final eligibility — ready for arbitrator assignment
case_statuses.FINAL_DETERMINATION_PENDING: Assigned to arbitrator — decision being deliberated
case_statuses.FINAL_DETERMINATION_RENDERED: Arbitrator has issued a binding decision
case_statuses.PENDING_CLOSURE_PAYMENTS: Post-decision — refunds and internal payouts being processed
case_statuses.PENDING_ADMINISTRATIVE_CLOSURE: Flagged by admin for manual closure
case_statuses.INELIGIBLE_PENDING_ADMIN_FEE: Failed eligibility but $115 admin fee still needs to be collected
case_statuses.INELIGIBLE: Terminal — case did not qualify for IDR process
case_statuses.CLOSED_INITIATING_PARTY: Terminal — initiating party (provider) won the arbitration
case_statuses.CLOSED_SPLIT_DECISION: Terminal — split decision, award divided between both parties
case_statuses.CLOSED_DEFAULT: Terminal — closed due to non-payment (legacy status)
case_statuses.CLOSED_DEFAULT_IP: Terminal — initiating party defaulted on payment
case_statuses.CLOSED_DEFAULT_NIP: Terminal — non-initiating party defaulted on payment
case_statuses.CLOSED_ADMINISTRATIVE: Terminal — closed by admin action after both parties paid
case_statuses.NOTICE_OF_DISMISSAL_NON_PAYMENT: Terminal — both parties failed to pay, case dismissed
case_statuses.PAYBACK_REQUEST: Refund correction state — overpayment being recovered
case_statuses.REOPENED_FOR_CORRECTION: Case reopened to correct a prior determination

payment_types.REFUND_TO_PREVAILING_PARTY: Entity fee refund to winning party (outgoing)
payment_types.THIRD_PARTY_PAYMENT: Internal payout to Halo or VeraTru

payment_directions.OUTGOING: Payments sent to parties or internal payees

refund_amounts.split_decision: $297.50 (50% of entity fee to each party)

payment_variance_types.OVERPAYMENT: Actual paid > expected amount — refund may be needed
payment_variance_types.UNDERPAYMENT: Actual paid < expected amount — collection needed

dispute_types.SINGLE: 1 line item — $710 total per party ($595 entity + $115 CMS)
dispute_types.BUNDLED: 2+ line items (not batched) — same pricing as SINGLE
dispute_types.BATCHED: 2-25+ line items explicitly batched — $910 base + surcharges per additional 25 items

party_types.INITIATING: The party that filed the dispute (typically healthcare provider)

arbitration_rules.decision_types: CASE_LEVEL (single award for entire case) or LINE_ITEM_LEVEL (per line item)
arbitration_rules.case_closure_after_decision: IP wins → CLOSED_INITIATING_PARTY, NIP wins → CLOSED_NON_INITIATING_PARTY, Split → CLOSED_SPLIT_DECISION
arbitration_rules.correction_workflow: Cases can be reopened (REOPENED_FOR_CORRECTION) before refunds complete — all pending refunds deleted, new determination rendered
arbitration_rules.arbitrator_assignment: Arbitrators self-assign from cases in FINAL_ELIGIBILITY_COMPLETED or FINAL_DETERMINATION_PENDING

closure_reasons.EARLY_RESUBMISSION: Early closure — party chose to resubmit
closure_reasons.EARLY_SETTLEMENT: Early closure — parties settled outside IDR

calculations.processing_time_days: CEIL((case.statusChangedAt - case.createdAt) / 86400000) — time from creation to current status in days
calculations.case_balance: SUM(payment_allocations where status IN ['COMPLETED','APPROVED']) - SUM(refunds where status='COMPLETED')
calculations.team_performance_cases_resolved: COUNT(cases in CLOSED_* or FINAL_DETERMINATION_RENDERED statuses)
calculations.urgency_scoring: Overdue = due_date < NOW(), Urgent = due_date within 2 business days, Warning = within 5 business days

existing_reports.name: Case Analytics
existing_reports.description: Case volume trends, status duration, resolution time, outcome analysis

existing_reports.name: Case Balance
existing_reports.description: Financial balance of each case as of a specific date — payments received minus refunds issued

existing_reports.name: CMS Payments Analytics
existing_reports.description: CMS payment amounts, case correlations, processing timeline trends

existing_reports.name: Daily Funds Incoming
existing_reports.description: Daily reconciliation of incoming payments and invoices

existing_reports.name: Daily Funds Outgoing
existing_reports.description: Daily reconciliation of outgoing disbursements and payouts

existing_reports.name: Daily Transactions
existing_reports.description: Transaction-level detail with 7-digit IDs for QuickBooks integration

existing_reports.name: Due Dates
existing_reports.description: Tracks case deadlines, overdue cases, urgency scoring, workload management

existing_reports.name: IDRE Payouts
existing_reports.description: Internal fee payouts to Halo, VeraTru, Capitol Bridge by dispute

existing_reports.name: Outstanding Payments
existing_reports.description: Disputes eligible for payment but not yet paid — tracking owed amounts

existing_reports.name: Payment Variance
existing_reports.description: Overpayments and underpayments needing refunds or collections

existing_reports.name: Team Performance
existing_reports.description: Arbitrator and staff productivity — cases resolved, resolution time, workload

existing_reports.name: Unpaid Disputes
existing_reports.description: Cases where at least one party hasn't paid — tracks payment status per party

existing_reports.name: Dashboard Stats
existing_reports.description: Quick stats: total cases, total payments, avg processing time, active arbitrators

platform_roles.master-admin: Full system access — all reports, user management, all tables
platform_roles.payment-admin: Payment and financial administration — all financial reports
platform_roles.admin-support: Case management, eligibility support, payment collections
platform_roles.arbitrator: Renders decisions on cases — can self-assign from eligible cases
platform_roles.arbitrator-manager: Manages arbitrator team — team performance reports
platform_roles.capitol-bridge-admin: Capitol Bridge fund management — outstanding payments

organization_rules.case_parties: Each case has an initiatingPartyOrganization (who filed) and nonInitiatingPartyOrganization (respondent)
organization_rules.case_ownership: ownedByOrganization = the organization that manages/owns the case
organization_rules.members: Organizations have members with roles: owner, admin, member
organization_rules.bank_accounts: Each org can have multiple bank accounts, each requiring approval workflow

key_table_relationships.case_to_payments: case → case_payment_allocation (caseId) → payment (paymentId) — tracks which payments fund which cases
key_table_relationships.case_to_parties: case → case_party (caseId) — links organizations as IP or NIP for a case
key_table_relationships.case_to_decisions: case → arbitration_decision (caseId) — one decision per case; line_item_decision for per-item decisions
key_table_relationships.case_to_refunds: case → case_refunds (caseId) — refund records tied to case outcomes
key_table_relationships.case_to_actions: case → case_action (caseId) — audit trail of all status changes and events
key_table_relationships.case_to_invoices: case → invoice_item (caseId) → invoice — billing for case fees
